summary: Small Linux build fix PR touching the root Makefile, obj_files.mk, and case-only source path renames. The changes make the build more portable on case-sensitive filesystems by correcting directory/path casing for Runtime and PPC_EABI, avoiding problematic Makefile directory discovery, allowing WINE to be overridden from the environment, and disabling built-in make suffix rules.
what_changed: Removed Makefile variables SRC_DIRS and ASM_DIRS that were populated with `find src/ -type f -name '*.c'` and `find asm/ -type f -name '*.s'`.
Changed WINE assignment on non-Windows builds from unconditional `WINE := wine` to overridable `WINE ?= wine`.
Added `.SUFFIXES:` with a comment indicating that all built-in make rules should be removed.

Changed ALL_DIRS creation from `build $(BUILD_DIR) $(addprefix $(BUILD_DIR)/,$(SRC_DIRS) $(ASM_DIRS))` to `$(sort $(dir $(O_FILES)))`, deriving required output directories from actual object file paths.
Corrected one object path in obj_files.mk from `src/MSL/ppc_eabi/critical_regions.gamecube.o` to `src/MSL/PPC_EABI/critical_regions.gamecube.o`.
Renamed runtime source/header paths from `src/runtime/...` to `src/Runtime/...` with 100% content similarity, indicating a case-only directory naming correction.
smart_moves: Derived build directories from `$(O_FILES)` instead of separately scanning source and asm trees, reducing the chance that directory creation gets out of sync with the object manifest.
Used `$(sort $(dir $(O_FILES)))` to both extract parent directories and deduplicate them before `mkdir -p`.
Made `WINE` overridable with `?=`, preserving the default while allowing Linux users or CI environments to inject a custom wine wrapper/path.
Explicitly cleared built-in suffix rules with `.SUFFIXES:`, which helps make behavior less dependent on host make defaults.
Fixed path casing in both the object manifest and source tree, which matters on Linux where `runtime` and `Runtime`, or `ppc_eabi` and `PPC_EABI`, are different paths.
decomp_lessons: For cross-platform decomp builds, object manifests and source directory names must match exact case; macOS or Windows may hide casing mistakes that break Linux.
Prefer deriving generated/build directory lists from the authoritative object-file list rather than independently walking source trees.
Makefile defaults for host tools should generally be overridable so contributors can adapt to local Wine/devkitPPC installations without editing tracked files.
Disabling implicit make rules can prevent host-specific or unintended recipes from interfering with carefully controlled decomp build steps.
Case-only renames are meaningful project hygiene in repositories that must build on case-sensitive filesystems.
naming_conventions: Runtime directory casing should be `src/Runtime`, not `src/runtime`, for the files shown in this PR.
MSL PPC EABI path casing should use `PPC_EABI`, as in `src/MSL/PPC_EABI/critical_regions.gamecube.o`.
Build variables in the root Makefile use uppercase names such as `BUILD_DIR`, `O_FILES`, `ALL_DIRS`, `WINE`, and `DEVKITPPC`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No function-level matching tactic is evidenced; this PR is a build portability fix rather than a decompilation or assembly-matching change.
The relevant build tactic is to base directory creation on the final object list so asm and C object outputs are covered consistently.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR context.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that modify ALL_DIRS or object-directory creation in the Makefile.
Search for remaining lowercase runtime or ppc_eabi path references that could break Linux builds.
Review whether other host tools in the Makefile use overridable `?=` defaults like WINE.
Look for later build-system changes involving `.SUFFIXES` or implicit make rule removal.
